grants.title: Adult Literacy Education (ALE) Grant
grants.summary: Funds programs that promote adult literacy and education in New York State.
grants.description: The Adult Literacy Education (ALE) Grant supports initiatives aimed at improving literacy skills among adult learners in New York State. The grant focuses on providing pathways to post-secondary education, apprenticeship training, and workforce integration. Eligible applicants include organizations that offer adult education programs, literacy zones, and employment preparation education. Applications must demonstrate alignment with state education goals and provide detailed plans for program implementation. Interested parties can apply through the New York State Education Department's official channels.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible organizations include those providing adult education programs, literacy zones, and employment preparation education in New York State.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Must operate within New York State
Provide adult education or literacy programs
Align with state education goals
Demonstrate capacity for program implementation

grants.title: 2025-26 Perkins Applications and 2024-25 Final Narrative Template
grants.summary: Funds career and technical education programs under the Perkins Act.
grants.description: This grant supports career and technical education (CTE) programs that provide academic and technical instruction in areas such as agriculture, business and marketing, health sciences, trade and technical education, and technology education. It aims to enhance CTE programs and ensure compliance with the Perkins Act. Eligible applicants must complete the Comprehensive Local Needs Assessment and submit the required application template. The grant benefits schools and organizations offering CTE programs to students.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible organizations include schools and educational institutions offering career and technical education programs.
key_requirements: Completion of Comprehensive Local Needs Assessment
Submission of 2024-25 Final Narrative Template
Compliance with Perkins Act guidelines

grants.title: Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA) Grant
grants.summary: Funds programs and initiatives to promote educational equity and ensure student success in New York State.
grants.description: The Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA) grant supports districts and schools in New York State to foster equity in education and ensure that every student succeeds. The grant emphasizes accountability, student success, and equitable services for nonpublic schools. Applicants can access resources such as technical assistance meetings, training sessions, and webinars to support their applications. The grant is administered by the New York State Education Department (NYSED), and applications are submitted through the Consolidated Application process.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include school districts, educational organizations, and nonpublic schools in New York State.
key_requirements: Applicants must be located in New York State.
Programs must align with ESSA goals of equity and student success.
Applications must be submitted through the Consolidated Application process.

grants.title: Funding for Public High School Equivalency (HSE) Test Administration
grants.summary: This grant provides funding for the administration of public High School Equivalency (HSE) tests in New York State.
grants.description: The grant supports the administration of public High School Equivalency (HSE) tests, ensuring access to educational opportunities for individuals seeking an HSE diploma. Eligible applicants include organizations involved in HSE test administration. Applications must be submitted by the specified deadline, and the project period spans three years. Detailed information and application forms are available on the NYSED website.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations involved in public High School Equivalency (HSE) test administration are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Must be an organization involved in HSE test administration
Applications must be submitted by the deadline
Compliance with NYSED guidelines

grants.title: New York State Indian Aid Program
grants.summary: Provides funding for Indigenous students attending postsecondary schools and assistance to public school districts educating Indigenous children on reservations.
grants.description: The New York State Indian Aid Program allocates state funding to support Indigenous students attending postsecondary schools and provides assistance to public school districts educating Indigenous children residing on reservations throughout New York State. The program aims to enhance educational opportunities and outcomes for Indigenous communities. Applicants can learn more and apply through the New York State Education Department's Office of Indigenous Education. Funding is distributed based on eligibility and program requirements.
grants.eligibility_notes: Indigenous students attending postsecondary schools and public school districts educating Indigenous children on reservations are eligible.
key_requirements: Applicants must be Indigenous students attending postsecondary schools.
Public school districts must educate Indigenous children residing on reservations.
Funding is allocated based on program eligibility.

grants.title: IDEA Grant Information
grants.summary: Funds programs and initiatives under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) to support students with disabilities.
grants.description: The IDEA Grant provides funding to support programs and services for students with disabilities across New York State. The grant aims to ensure equal opportunities and positive outcomes for these students by funding initiatives such as technical assistance, professional development, and policy guidance. Eligible applicants include organizations and schools working with students with disabilities. Applications can be submitted through the New York State Education Department's website.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations and schools working with students with disabilities are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Must be an organization or school working with students with disabilities
Must comply with IDEA regulations
Must operate within New York State

grants.title: VITAL Educator Program
grants.summary: Funds initiatives to support educators in integrating technology effectively in P-12 educational environments.
grants.description: The VITAL Educator Program aims to enhance teaching and learning by promoting the effective integration of technology in P-12 educational settings across New York State. This program supports educators through resources, training, and initiatives designed to improve digital learning practices. Applications are currently being accepted, and interested educators can apply directly through the provided application link. The program benefits educators and students by fostering innovative teaching methods and improving educational outcomes.
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to educators in New York State working in P-12 educational environments.
key_requirements: Applicants must be educators in New York State.
Must work in P-12 educational environments.
Focus on integrating technology into teaching practices.

grants.title: Updated Mandated Reporter Training Requirements
grants.summary: This grant funds updated training for mandated reporters in New York State to include protocols for reducing implicit bias, identifying adverse childhood experiences, and recognizing signs of abuse or maltreatment during virtual interactions.
grants.description: The Updated Mandated Reporter Training is required for mandated reporters in New York State, including educators, school leaders, and other professionals. The training includes strategies to reduce implicit bias, identify adverse childhood experiences, and recognize signs of abuse or maltreatment during virtual interactions. Mandated reporters who have previously completed the training must complete the updated curriculum by April 1, 2025. Approved training providers must report completion electronically to the Office of Teaching Initiatives. Applications for licensure, certification, or registration require proof of completion.
grants.eligibility_notes: Mandated reporters in New York State, including educators, school leaders, and other professionals, are eligible.
sdg_alignment: SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
key_requirements: Completion of updated Mandated Reporter Training curriculum
Training must be reported electronically by NYSED-approved providers
Required for registration or reregistration of teaching status every five years
Required for initial application for licensure, certification, or limited permit
Required for earning a college recommendation on or after April 1, 2025

grants.title: Art & Music Teacher Salary Reimbursement Grant (AMTS) Year 2
grants.summary: Funds reimbursement for salaries of art and music teachers in religious and independent schools.
grants.description: The AMTS Year 2 grant provides financial support to religious and independent schools in New York State by reimbursing salaries for art and music teachers. This initiative aims to enhance educational opportunities in these schools and support their operational needs. Eligible schools can submit claims electronically through the NYSED business portal. The grant is part of broader efforts to assist religious and independent schools in their operations and professional development.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are religious and independent schools in New York State.
key_requirements: Must be a religious or independent school in New York State
Must employ art and music teachers
Submission of reimbursement claims through the NYSED business portal

grants.title: New York State Technical and Education Assistance Center for Homeless Students
grants.summary: Funds the establishment of a technical and education assistance center to support homeless students in New York State.
grants.description: This grant supports the creation of a center to provide technical and educational assistance for homeless students across New York State. The center will aim to improve access to education, provide resources, and address challenges faced by homeless students. Eligible applicants must demonstrate capacity to manage such a center and provide detailed plans for implementation. Applications must be submitted through the specified process outlined on the opportunity page.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with the capacity to establish and manage a technical and education assistance center for homeless students.
key_requirements: Capacity to manage a technical and education assistance center
Experience in supporting homeless students
Detailed implementation plan

grants.title: Teacher and Principal Evaluation: Teacher and Principal Practice Rubric
grants.summary: This grant supports the evaluation of teachers and principals using approved practice rubrics.
grants.description: The grant aims to improve teacher and principal evaluations by funding the use of standardized practice rubrics. It benefits schools and educational institutions seeking to enhance evaluation processes. Applications must adhere to the guidelines provided by the New York State Education Department. The grant supports efforts to ensure fair and effective evaluations in alignment with state standards.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include schools and educational institutions within New York State.
key_requirements: Applicants must be located in New York State.
Applicants must be schools or educational institutions.
Applications must align with NYSED guidelines.

grants.title: Request for Bid (RFB): New York State (NYS) Statewide Longitudinal Data System (SLDS) Data Governance Program Development Support Statement of Work (SOW)
grants.summary: This grant funds the development and support of a data governance program for the New York State Statewide Longitudinal Data System (SLDS).
grants.description: The grant aims to support the creation and implementation of a data governance program for the New York State Statewide Longitudinal Data System (SLDS). The program will benefit educational institutions and stakeholders by improving data management and accessibility. Applicants are expected to provide expertise in project management and data governance. Interested parties can apply through the provided procurement process.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with expertise in data governance and project management are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Expertise in data governance
Experience in project management
Ability to support statewide educational data systems

grants.eligibility_notes: Certified Minority/Women Business Enterprises (M/WBE) are eligible to participate in state contracts.
key_requirements: Certified as a Minority/Women Business Enterprise (M/WBE)
Compliance with NYSED participation goals
Submission of required M/WBE forms

grants.title: Library Services and Technology Act (LSTA) Competitive Grant Program
grants.summary: This grant funds public libraries and library systems to improve services and resources in areas such as outreach, literacy, and community engagement.
grants.description: The Library Services and Technology Act (LSTA) Competitive Grant Program aims to enhance the capacity of public libraries and library systems to provide services and information resources to their communities. The grant focuses on outreach, literacy best practices, and social wellbeing to improve the lives of New Yorkers. Eligible applicants include library systems and public and association libraries chartered by the Regents. Applications must be submitted through the State Library’s online grant application system, and prequalification in the Statewide Financial System (SFS) is required for not-for-profit applicants.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are library systems and public and association libraries chartered by the Regents. Applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System (SFS) and have a Unique Entity Identifier (UEI).
key_requirements: Applicants must be library systems or public/association libraries chartered by the Regents.
Applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System (SFS).
Applicants must have a Unique Entity Identifier (UEI).
Applications must be submitted through the State Library’s online grant application system.
Proposals must be received by the deadline of May 15, 2026.

grants.title: Extended School Day / School Violence Prevention Grant 2026-2031
grants.summary: Funds programs that provide extended school day activities and/or school safety initiatives to promote violence prevention.
grants.description: The Extended School Day/School Violence Prevention (ESD/SVP) Program awards competitive grants to support students through extended school day activities and school safety programs that promote violence prevention. Programs must align with school safety plans required by New York State Education Law §2801-a and should not replace existing after-school funding. Eligible applicants include public school districts and not-for-profit organizations working in collaboration with public school districts. Applications must be submitted electronically through Survey Monkey Apply by the deadline.
grants.award_text: Minimum $125,000 for ESD grants; maximum $450,000 annually per applicant.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include public school districts and not-for-profit organizations working in partnership with one or more public school districts.
sdg_alignment: SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
key_requirements: Applicants must be public school districts or not-for-profit organizations working in partnership with public school districts.
Programs must align with school safety plans required by New York State Education Law §2801-a.
Applications must be submitted electronically through Survey Monkey Apply.
Nonprofit applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System (SFS) by the proposal due date.
Funding requests cannot exceed $450,000 annually per applicant.

grants.title: 2026-27 Local Government Records Management Improvement Fund (LGRMIF) Competitive Grant Awards
grants.summary: Funds to help local governments establish or improve records management programs.
grants.description: The Local Government Records Management Improvement Fund (LGRMIF) grants program provides competitive funding to assist local governments in establishing records management programs or developing new components. Created in 1989, the fund is derived from fees collected by county clerks and the NYC Register for document recording and court case indexing. Grants are awarded based on application merit, and local governments are expected to assume ongoing program support. Eligible applicants include local authorities, community colleges, fire districts, libraries, and more. Applications must be submitted electronically via the LGRMIF eGrants system.
grants.award_text: Up to $75,000 for individual grants; up to $150,000 for shared services grants; up to $10,000 for disaster management projects.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include local governments such as local authorities, community colleges, fire districts, volunteer fire companies, ambulance services, libraries, NYC Department of Records and Information Services (DORIS), and the NYS Board of Education.
eligible_applicant_types: NYC Department of Records and Information Services (DORIS)
key_requirements: Applicants must be local governments or eligible entities as defined in the RFP.
Applications must be submitted electronically via the LGRMIF eGrants system.
Paper signature forms must be postmarked by the application deadline.
Not-for-profit vendors must prequalify in the Statewide Financial System (SFS) before applying.

grants.title: 2026 Title I, Section 1003 School Improvement Grant (SIG) – Planning
grants.summary: Funds planning and development of district and school improvement plans for eligible districts and schools under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA).
grants.description: The 2026 Title I, Section 1003 School Improvement Grant (SIG) – Planning provides funding to support the development and implementation of district and school continuous improvement plans as required under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA). Eligible districts and schools include newly identified Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). Funds can be used for staff participation in needs assessments, parent/family engagement, travel to NYSED regional training, and planning support. Applications must be submitted through the NYSED Business Portal, and all costs must be encumbered during the project period.
grants.award_text: $20,000 per district or $20,000 per school
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include newly identified Target Districts and schools identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI. Charter schools and districts with only schools identified for Local Support and Improvement (LSI) are not eligible.
eligible_applicant_types: Schools identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI
key_requirements: Must be a newly identified Target District or school identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI.
Application must be submitted through the NYSED Business Portal.
Funds must be used for eligible activities such as needs assessments, parent/family engagement, or planning support.
All costs must be encumbered during the project period.

grants.title: 2026-27 Conservation/Preservation Discretionary Grants
grants.summary: Funds projects that contribute to the preservation of significant research materials in libraries, archives, historical societies, and similar agencies within New York State.
grants.description: The grant aims to encourage proper care and accessibility of research materials in New York State, promote guidelines and standards for conservation/preservation work, and support local and cooperative preservation programs. Eligible projects include improving collection storage environments, reformatting or treating collections, and other preservation activities. Applications must be submitted through the Online Grant Application System by the deadline. Nonprofit applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System (SFS) to be considered.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include agencies and libraries chartered by the Regents, not-for-profit libraries, archives, historical societies, and consortia or cooperating groups of such agencies within New York State.
key_requirements: Applicants must be chartered by the Regents or be not-for-profit organizations.
Projects must focus on preservation of significant research materials.
Applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System (SFS).
Applications must be submitted through the Online Grant Application System.
No financial match is required, but institutional contribution of staff time is necessary.

grants.title: 2025-2026 My Brother's Keeper Fellows Program
grants.summary: This grant funds leadership opportunities and service projects for rising 12th-grade students, with an emphasis on boys and young men of color.
grants.description: The My Brother's Keeper Fellows Program provides rising 12th-grade students, particularly boys and young men of color, with authentic leadership experiences and opportunities to develop service projects that benefit their schools and communities. The program involves partnerships between NYSMBK Communities and postsecondary institutions or local businesses/community organizations. Selected Fellows will participate in internships, receive mentorship, and contribute to a statewide MBK Fellows Workgroup. Applications require a Memorandum of Agreement signed by all partners, including municipal officials, school district superintendents, and postsecondary institutions or local organizations.
grants.award_text: $5,950 per Fellow per year
grants.eligibility_notes: Only approved New York State My Brother’s Keeper Community Networks (NYSMBKCN) may apply. Applicants must secure a Memorandum of Agreement with local postsecondary institutions or local businesses/community organizations.
eligible_applicant_types: Public school districts within NYSMBK Community Networks
key_requirements: Applicants must be approved NYSMBK Community Networks.
Applications must include a signed Memorandum of Agreement with partners.
Partners must include municipal officials, school district superintendents, and postsecondary institutions or local organizations.
Students must be rising 12th graders enrolled in NYSMBK Community Networks.

grants.title: 2025-26 Title I, 1003 School Improvement Grant (District Systems for Instructional Improvement)
grants.summary: Provides funding to Target Districts with schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI) to participate in the NYSED District Systems for Instructional Improvement Supplemental Support program.
grants.description: The grant aims to support Target Districts with schools identified for CSI, ATSI, and TSI models to improve instructional systems. Eligible districts must have completed the Notice of Intent and meet specific criteria. Funding supports school teams, including principals, instructional coaches, and teachers, to attend training, purchase professional resources, and hold team meetings. Applications must include a signed FS-10 Budget form and be submitted to the NYSED Office of Accountability. Budget amendments are allowed until June 1, 2026.
grants.award_text: Up to $50,000 per school team and up to $125,000 per instructional coach (maximum of two coaches per team).
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are Target Districts with schools identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI models that have completed the Notice of Intent and meet specific criteria.
key_requirements: Completion of the District Systems for Instructional Improvement Notice of Intent.
At least one school in CSI, ATSI, or TSI support models enrolled in the Supplemental Support program.
Team composition including principal supervisor, principals, instructional coaches, and teachers.
Instructional coaches must have prior experience and meet specific training requirements.

grants.title: Charter Schools Program (CSP) Expansion Grants
grants.summary: Funds the expansion of high-quality educational opportunities by increasing seats in existing charter schools in New York State.
grants.description: The Charter Schools Program (CSP) Expansion Grants aim to support the expansion of high-quality educational opportunities for students in New York State. The grant focuses on increasing the number of seats in existing charter schools that provide significant educational benefits, especially for students at risk of not meeting State academic standards. Eligible applicants include charter school education corporations authorized by New York State charter entities. Applications must be submitted electronically, and prequalification in the Statewide Financial System is required. Funding beyond Year One is contingent upon legislative appropriations and grantee progress.
grants.award_text: Up to $10,000,000 with no more than 5 grants to be awarded
grants.eligibility_notes: Existing charter school education corporations authorized by New York State charter entities, with approval to significantly expand enrollment or add grade levels.
key_requirements: Applicant must be an existing charter school education corporation.
Charter schools must be authorized by New York State charter entities.
Approval to significantly expand enrollment or add grade levels is required.
Prequalification in the Statewide Financial System is mandatory.
Applications must be submitted electronically by the deadline.

grants.title: RFP 52: Charter Schools Programs (CSP) Start-Up Grants
grants.summary: Provides funding to new charter schools for planning, program design, and initial implementation.
grants.description: This grant supports new charter schools authorized by the Board of Regents or the SUNY Board of Trustees. It funds planning, program design, and initial implementation activities during the pre-opening phase or the first five years of operation. Eligible applicants must be charter school education corporations applying on behalf of a new charter school they operate. Applications must be submitted electronically, and prequalification in the Statewide Financial System is required. The grant period runs from September 1, 2025, to August 31, 2028.
grants.award_text: Up to $28,000,000 with no more than 14 grants awarded
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are charter school education corporations operating a new charter school authorized by the Board of Regents or the SUNY Board of Trustees. The school must be in its planning phase or initial five years of operation and cannot have received a CSP grant for the same purpose within the last five years.
key_requirements: Must be a charter school education corporation
School must be authorized by the Board of Regents or SUNY Board of Trustees
School must be in planning phase or initial five years of operation
Cannot have received a CSP grant for the same purpose within the last five years
Prequalification in the Statewide Financial System is required

grants.title: 2025-26 Title I 1003 School Improvement Grant (Coaching for Excellence)
grants.summary: Funds instructional coaching positions for schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI) or Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI).
grants.description: The 2025-26 Title I 1003 School Improvement Grant (Coaching for Excellence) provides funding to Target Districts with schools identified for CSI or ATSI to support the employment of instructional coaches. The grant aims to enhance educational outcomes through participation in the NYSED Coaching for Excellence Supplemental Support program. Eligible schools can apply for funding to cover salary and benefits for up to two full-time instructional coaches. Applications must meet program-specific criteria, and funding is limited to costs associated with the coaching positions. Applications are submitted through the NYSED Business Portal.
grants.award_text: Up to $125,000 per instructional coach
grants.eligibility_notes: Target Districts with at least one non-Receivership school in the CSI or ATSI support model are eligible.
key_requirements: School must be identified for CSI or ATSI support model.
Instructional coach must meet experience criteria (at least 5 years teaching experience).
Coach must not have served as an administrator during 2023-24 or 2024-25.
School must address staffing needs arising from moving teacher to coach position.
School must fulfill Coaching for Excellence Program Participation Expectations.

grants.title: 2025-26 Title I, 1003 School Improvement Grant (Basic)
grants.summary: Funds to support the development, implementation, and monitoring of district and school improvement plans.
grants.description: The 2025-26 Title I, 1003 School Improvement Grant (Basic) provides funding to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) identified for additional support through the accountability system. The grant supports the implementation and monitoring of strategies in the District Comprehensive Improvement Plan (DCIP) and School Comprehensive Educational Plan(s) (SCEP), as well as the planning and development of 2026-27 improvement plans. LEAs must submit applications through the NYSED Business Portal and adhere to specific submission requirements. Funds must be encumbered during the project period, and budget amendments must be submitted by May 15, 2026.
grants.award_text: District allocations are determined based on the number of schools identified for TSI, ATSI, CSI, and other programs.
grants.eligibility_notes: Target districts are eligible. Districts identified for Local Support and Improvement (LSI), schools identified for LSI, and charter schools are not eligible.
key_requirements: LEAs must complete all required questions in the application.
Applications must be submitted through the NYSED Business Portal.
FS-10 Budget forms must be sent in hard copy with blue ink signatures.
Budget amendments must be submitted by May 15, 2026.

grants.title: FY2024 National School Lunch Program Equipment Assistance Grant for School Food Authorities
grants.summary: This grant provides funding for schools to purchase equipment to improve meal quality, food safety, and expand participation in school meal programs.
grants.description: The FY2024 National School Lunch Program Equipment Assistance Grant aims to enhance the infrastructure of the National School Lunch Program (NSLP) by funding equipment purchases for schools. The grant supports healthier meals, improved food safety, and expanded participation in school meal programs, including the School Breakfast Program. Eligible applicants include School Food Authorities (SFAs) in New York State that were not awarded this grant in FY2020, FY2021, FY2022, or FY2023. Applications must be submitted via the designated SharePoint site by the deadline, and each application must request equipment valued at over $1,000. SFAs must obligate funds by September 30, 2025, and complete all procurement and expenditure activities by June 30, 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are School Food Authorities (SFAs) in New York State that were not awarded this grant in FY2020, FY2021, FY2022, or FY2023. SFAs must participate in the NSLP and submit separate applications for each piece of equipment requested.
grants.contact_name: Tara Webster, Elizabeth O'Conner, Thomas McBride
key_requirements: Applicants must be SFAs in New York State that were not awarded this grant in FY2020, FY2021, FY2022, or FY2023.
Applications must request equipment valued at over $1,000.
Applications must be submitted via the designated SharePoint site by the deadline.
SFAs must obligate funds by September 30, 2025.
Procurement and expenditure activities must be completed by June 30, 2026.

grants.title: RFP #82: McKinney-Vento Subgrant (2025-2028)
grants.summary: Funds to support local educational agencies in promoting school success for homeless children and youth.
grants.description: The McKinney-Vento Subgrant provides funding to local educational agencies (LEAs) in New York State to develop and implement strategies and model programs that facilitate the enrollment, attendance, and success in school of homeless children and youth. Funds may be used for educational services, expedited evaluations, awareness training, health services, transportation, early childhood programs, record keeping, parent programs, coordinating services, violence prevention, and providing supplies and services at shelters and temporary housing facilities. LEAs must address students' basic, academic, and unique needs in their proposals. Applications must be submitted electronically, and prequalification in the Statewide Financial System is required for nonprofit applicants.
grants.award_text: Total funding of $22,500,000.00
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are New York State local educational agencies (LEAs), including public school districts, charter schools, and BOCES. LEAs must have identified a minimum average of 100 students in temporary housing over the past three school years or apply as part of a consortium to meet this threshold.
key_requirements: LEAs must have identified a minimum average of 100 students in temporary housing over the past three school years.
BOCES may only apply as part of a consortium.
Consortium members must be direct providers of services and cannot include third-party contractors.
Nonprofit applicants must be prequalified in the Statewide Financial System by the application deadline.
Applications must be submitted electronically by the deadline.

grants.title: Advanced Course Access (ACA) Program (2025-2027)
grants.summary: Funds programs to increase equity in access to advanced courses for high school students with limited or no access.
grants.description: The ACA Program aims to provide high school students with equitable access to advanced courses such as Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and dual-credit courses. This is achieved through virtual instruction, blended learning, or other evidence-based methods. Eligible applicants include BOCES-led consortia and Big 5 public school districts. Applications must include a budget for Year One and a two-year budget narrative. Funding beyond Year One is contingent upon legislative appropriations, satisfactory performance, and timely reporting.
grants.award_text: Up to $500,000 annually
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include BOCES-led consortia and Big 5 public school districts. Individual schools and charter schools are not eligible to apply independently.
key_requirements: Applicants must be BOCES-led consortia or Big 5 public school districts.
BOCES-led consortia must include at least one BOCES and six Rest of State districts.
Funding beyond Year One is contingent upon legislative appropriations and satisfactory performance.
Applications must include a budget for Year One and a two-year budget narrative.
Programs must address systemic barriers to advanced coursework for marginalized groups.

grants.title: 2025 Title I, 1003 School Improvement Grant (SIG) Supplemental Support
grants.summary: Funds provided to support Target Districts and schools identified for improvement models to extend coaching and concepts for continuous improvement plans.
grants.description: The 2025 Title I, 1003 School Improvement Grant (SIG) Supplemental Support provides funding to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) for Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). The funds aim to extend coaching and concepts provided during NYSED’s Supplemental Support programs in preparation for the 2025-26 school year. Eligible schools must complete a School Narrative and School Spending Plan as part of the application process. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and funds must comply with federal guidelines.
grants.award_text: $20,000 per eligible school
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible Target Districts must have at least one school identified in the CSI, ATSI, or TSI support model that participated in Enhanced Principal Leadership (EPL), Assistant Principal Mentoring (APM), and/or Coaching for Excellence (CFE) programs in 2024-25.
key_requirements: Target Districts must have at least one school identified in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models.
Schools must have participated in EPL, APM, or CFE programs in 2024-25.
Applications must include a School Narrative and School Spending Plan.
Funds must comply with federal guidelines.

grants.title: 2025 Title I, Section 1003 School Improvement Grant – Planning
grants.summary: Funds planning and development of district and school improvement plans for eligible districts and schools under the ESEA.
grants.description: The 2025 Title I, Section 1003 School Improvement Grant (SIG) Planning provides funding to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) for Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). The funds support the development and implementation of continuous improvement plans for the 2025–2026 school year. Eligible districts and schools can use the funds for needs assessments, family participation, surveys, and planning support. Applications must be submitted online through the NYSED Business Portal, and detailed instructions are provided in the grant announcement files.
grants.award_text: $20,000 per district or school
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible districts include newly identified Target Districts. Eligible schools include those previously identified for Local Support and Improvement (LSI) with at least one subgroup in TSI. Charter schools and districts with only LSI schools are not eligible.
key_requirements: Must be a newly identified Target District or eligible school
Funds must be used for planning activities such as needs assessments and surveys
Applications must be submitted online through the NYSED Business Portal
FS-10 budget form must align proposed expenses to planning activities
No carryover of funds; all costs must be encumbered during the project period

grants.title: 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Resource Allocation Review)
grants.summary: Funds to support school improvement in districts with schools identified for additional supports, promoting equitable resource distribution.
grants.description: The 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant (SIG) 1003 Resource Allocation Review (RAR) is a pilot program for the 2024-25 school year provided by the New York State Education Department (NYSED). It aims to promote equitable resource distribution and support districts in meeting federal requirements regarding resource equity. Funds are allocated to districts with a significant number of schools identified for Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI). Eligible districts must submit an FS-10 budget form and meet specific criteria. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and funds must be used for activities directly related to the program's goals.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible Target Districts must have at least five schools, with at least 20% identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI. Additional criteria include economic disadvantage thresholds.
key_requirements: District must have at least five schools.
At least 20% of schools must be identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI.
Economic disadvantage thresholds must be met.

grants.title: 2024-25 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Targeted Support)
grants.summary: Funds provided to Target Districts with schools under Receivership for additional support and technical assistance to meet annual targets.
grants.description: The 2024-25 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Targeted Support) aims to assist Target Districts with schools under Receivership that participate in Targeted Coaching. The grant provides $200,000 per eligible school to support activities and expenses aligned with Demonstrable Improvement Indicators (DIIs) and Lead Strategies. Eligible districts must submit a School Spending Plan, FS-10 budget form, and Continuation Plan for each participating school. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and funds must comply with federal 'supplement, not supplant' guidelines.
grants.award_text: $200,000 per eligible school
grants.eligibility_notes: Target Districts with at least one eligible school under Receivership registered for Targeted Coaching.
key_requirements: Target Districts must have at least one eligible school under Receivership.
Schools must be registered for Targeted Coaching.
Submission of School Spending Plan, FS-10 budget form, and Continuation Plan.

grants.title: 2024-25 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Basic)
grants.summary: Funds for Local Education Agencies (LEAs) to support district and school improvement plans under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA).
grants.description: The 2024-25 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Basic) provides funding to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) for Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). The grant supports the development, implementation, and monitoring of district and school continuous improvement plans to improve student performance. Eligible districts and schools will receive allocations based on their designation. Applications must be submitted through the NYSED Business Portal, and FS-10 Budget forms are required. Amendments to budgets must be submitted by August 1, 2025.
grants.award_text: $50,000 per Target District, $250,000 per school in CSI, $150,000 per school in ATSI, $75,000 per school in TSI
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). Charter schools and districts/schools identified for Local Support and Improvement are not eligible.
key_requirements: LEAs must complete all required questions marked with a red asterisk.
FS-10 Budget forms must clearly identify allocation sources.
Applications must be submitted/certified by the chief school officer of the applicant LEA.
Blue ink signed original and two hard copies of FS-10 Budget forms must be sent to NYSED Office of Accountability.

grants.title: 2025-27 Learning Technology Grant
grants.summary: Funds innovative programs utilizing learning technologies to personalize learning and increase access to high-quality learning experiences, as well as professional development for educators.
grants.description: The 2025-27 Learning Technology Grant aims to develop, implement, and share innovative programs that utilize learning technologies to personalize learning and increase access to high-quality, rigorous learning experiences, including online, distance, or blended learning. It also supports professional development programs to assist teachers and educational leaders in effectively using learning technology to enhance teaching and learning. The grant focuses on improving culturally and linguistically responsive learning environments and aligns with the mission of the NYS Board of Regents to ensure equitable access to high-quality educational opportunities. Eligible applicants must submit a budget for Year One and a 2.5-year budget summary. Funding beyond Year One is contingent upon legislative appropriations, satisfactory performance, and timely reporting.
grants.award_text: Up to $100,000 annually for individual districts; up to $200,000 annually for consortia.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are public school districts and consortia of public school districts and BOCES. Non-public schools and charter schools are not eligible to apply directly but may participate through public school district proposals.
key_requirements: Applicants must be public school districts or consortia of public school districts and BOCES.
Religious and independent schools within the applicant's boundaries must be given the opportunity to participate.
Applicants must submit a budget for Year One and a 2.5-year budget summary.
Funding beyond Year One is contingent upon legislative appropriations, satisfactory performance, and timely reporting.

grants.title: 2024-25 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 Coaching for Excellence
grants.summary: Funds instructional coaching positions in schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI).
grants.description: The New York State Education Department (NYSED) provides funding to Target Districts with schools identified for CSI, ATSI, and TSI models to support the employment of instructional coaches. The grant aims to improve instructional practices through the Coaching for Excellence program. Eligible schools must meet specific criteria, including not having an instructional coach in the prior year and agreeing to program participation expectations. Applications must be submitted by July 15, 2024, and funding covers salary and benefits for instructional coaches.
grants.award_text: Up to $125,000 for new coaches; up to $50,000 for returning coaches.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible schools must be in Target Districts identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI models and meet specific criteria, including not having an instructional coach in the prior year.
key_requirements: School must be in a Target District identified for CSI, ATSI, or TSI models.
School must not have had an instructional coach in the prior year.
School must agree to program participation expectations.
School must finalize staffing coverage for the instructional coach position by September 2, 2024.

grants.title: 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 Supplemental Support
grants.summary: Funds provided to support Target Districts and schools identified for improvement to extend concepts and coaching for continuous improvement plans.
grants.description: The 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 Supplemental Support provides funding to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) for Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). The grant supports the extension of concepts and coaching provided to participants in NYSED’s Supplemental Support programs in preparation for the 2024-25 school year. Eligible districts must submit applications including spending plans and budgets. Funds must comply with federal guidelines and be used for professional learning or initiative development identified by school participants.
grants.award_text: $20,000 per school
grants.eligibility_notes: Target Districts with at least one school identified in CSI, ATSI, or TSI support models that participated in NYSED’s coaching programs in 2023-24 are eligible.
key_requirements: Target Districts must have at least one school identified in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models.
Schools must have participated in NYSED coaching programs in 2023-24.
Applications must include a School Spending Plan and FS-10 budget form.
Funds must align with federal guidelines and be used for professional learning or initiative development.

grants.title: FY 2023 National School Lunch Program Equipment Assistance Grant for School Food Authorities
grants.summary: This grant provides funding for schools to purchase equipment to improve meal quality, food safety, and participation in school meal programs.
grants.description: The FY 2023 National School Lunch Program Equipment Assistance Grant aims to enhance the infrastructure of the National School Lunch Program (NSLP). Schools can use the funding to purchase equipment that supports serving healthier meals, improving food safety, expanding participation in meal programs, and establishing or expanding the School Breakfast Program. Eligible applicants must submit a completed application form and required documentation via the SharePoint submission site by the deadline. Non-public and charter schools must prequalify to apply.
grants.award_text: Up to $20,000 per school food authority
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include school food authorities (SFAs), public schools, non-public schools, charter schools, and residential childcare programs.
key_requirements: Applicants must be school food authorities (SFAs).
Non-public and charter schools must prequalify by the application deadline.
Applications must include all required documentation as outlined in the RFP.
Equipment acquisition cost cannot exceed $20,000 per award.
Applications must be submitted via the SharePoint submission site.

grants.title: 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Planning)
grants.summary: Funds provided to support the planning and development of district and school improvement plans for the 2024–25 school year under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA).
grants.description: The 2024 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 (Planning) allocates funds to Local Education Agencies (LEAs) for Target Districts and schools identified for Comprehensive Support and Improvement (CSI), Additional Targeted Support and Improvement (ATSI), and Targeted Support and Improvement (TSI). These funds are intended to support the development and implementation of continuous improvement plans at the district and school levels. Eligible districts and schools can use the funds for activities such as needs assessments, family participation, surveys, and planning support. Applications must be submitted online through the NYSED Business Portal and certified by the chief school officer of the applicant LEA.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include LEAs for Target Districts and schools identified for CSI, ATSI, and TSI. Charter schools and previously identified Target Districts are not eligible.
eligible_applicant_types: Schools identified for CSI, ATSI, and TSI
key_requirements: LEAs must complete all sections of the online application.
Applications must be certified by the chief school officer of the applicant LEA.
FS-10 budget form must align proposed expenses to planning activities.
Funds must be encumbered during the project period (February 1, 2024 to August 31, 2024).
No carryover of funds is allowed.

grants.title: 2023-2024 Statewide Universal Full-Day Prekindergarten (SUFDPK) Expansion Grant
grants.summary: Funds to increase availability of full-day prekindergarten placements for four-year-old students in New York State school districts.
grants.description: The 2023-2024 SUFDPK Expansion Grant aims to increase the availability of high-quality prekindergarten programming for four-year-old students in New York State. Eligible school districts can use the funds to create new full-day placements or convert existing half-day placements to full-day. Programming must align with New York State Learning Standards and Education Law §3602-ee requirements, addressing student and community needs. Applications must be submitted by the deadline, and eligibility requirements are outlined in the RFP.
grants.award_text: $10,000 or $7,000 per pupil for new or converted seats
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to all public school districts in New York State. Community-based organizations (CBOs) cannot apply directly but may collaborate with school districts.
key_requirements: Must be a public school district in New York State
Funds must be used for new full-day placements or conversion of half-day placements to full-day
Programming must align with New York State Learning Standards
Must address student and community needs

grants.title: 2023-24 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 - Targeted Support for Long-term Identified Schools Grant
grants.summary: This grant provides funding to Target Districts with schools in the Receivership support model to assist them in meeting annual improvement targets.
grants.description: The New York State Education Department (NYSED) offers this grant to Target Districts with schools in the Receivership support model that participate in Targeted Coaching. The funds aim to provide additional support and technical assistance to help these schools meet their annual identified targets. Eligible districts must submit a School Spending Plan, FS-10 budget form, and Continuation Plan for each participating school. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and funds must align with Demonstrable Improvement Indicators and federal guidelines.
grants.award_text: $125,000 per eligible school
grants.eligibility_notes: Target Districts with at least one school in the Receivership program that has registered for Targeted Coaching are eligible.
key_requirements: District must have at least one school in the Receivership program.
School must participate in Targeted Coaching.
Funds must align with Demonstrable Improvement Indicators.
Submission of School Spending Plan, FS-10 budget form, and Continuation Plan.

grants.title: 2023-24 Title I School Improvement Grant 1003 High School Redesign Continuation
grants.summary: Funds for Target Districts with schools in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models to continue High School Redesign efforts.
grants.description: The New York State Education Department provides funding to Target Districts with schools in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models that participated in the 2022-23 High School Redesign Program. The grant supports activities such as convening High School Redesign teams, implementing High School Redesign Plans (HSRP) or School Comprehensive Education Plans (SCEP), and aligning spending plans with budgets. Applications require submission of specific documents and compliance with federal guidelines.
grants.award_text: $100,000 per school
grants.eligibility_notes: Target Districts with at least one school in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models that participated in the 2022-23 High School Redesign Program.
key_requirements: Schools must be in CSI, ATSI, or TSI models.
Schools must have participated in the 2022-23 High School Redesign Program.
Submission of signed application cover page and required documents.
Alignment of spending plans with HSRP or SCEP.
Compliance with federal 'supplement, not supplant' guidelines.

grants.title: 4201 Capital Grant Program: State-Supported Schools for the Blind and Deaf Facility Grant Program
grants.summary: This grant funds capital projects for state-supported schools for the blind and deaf, prioritizing health and safety needs.
grants.description: The 4201 Capital Grant Program provides funding for capital projects at state-supported schools for the blind and deaf in New York. The program prioritizes health and safety needs and allocates funds based on a plan developed by the State Education Department and approved by the Division of the Budget. Applicants can submit proposals for projects under $500,000 or over $500,000, with emergency funding available throughout the grant period. Applications must include detailed budgets and meet eligibility requirements. Emergency applications can be submitted outside the regular cycle, and funding is awarded based on need.
grants.award_text: $12.5M for projects under $500k and $12.5M for projects over $500k
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are state-supported schools for the blind and deaf in New York.
eligible_applicant_types: State-supported schools for the blind and deaf
key_requirements: Projects must address health and safety needs.
Applicants must be state-supported schools for the blind and deaf.
Applications must include detailed budgets and cost estimates.
Emergency applications require evidence of need and supporting documentation.
Projects must be completed within the grant term.

grants.title: 2023-2024 Charter School Expansion Grants
grants.summary: Supports the expansion of high-quality educational opportunities by increasing the number of seats in existing charter schools in New York State.
grants.description: This grant aims to expand the number of seats in existing charter schools in New York State that are already providing significant educational benefits to students, especially those at the greatest risk of not meeting State academic standards. Eligible applicants include charter school education corporations operating one or more charter schools that meet specific eligibility criteria, such as having their charter renewed at least once. Funds must be encumbered within the grant period, and applicants must submit annual reports to demonstrate enrollment increases. Applications are submitted through the New York State Education Department's process.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are charter school education corporations operating one or more charter schools that have had their charter renewed at least once by their authorizer. Pre-K programs and construction projects are not eligible.
key_requirements: Charter school must have had its charter renewed at least once by its authorizer.
Funds must be encumbered within the grant period (October 1, 2023 - September 30, 2024).
Annual reporting to NYSED’s Basic Education Reporting System (BEDS) is required.
Pre-K programs and construction projects are not eligible.

grants.title: Rethink Grant
grants.summary: Funds initiatives to foster high-quality public education options for students, families, and communities.
grants.description: The Rethink Grant supports projects aimed at improving public education through strong monitoring, oversight, and effective practice sharing. It is designed to benefit students, families, and communities by providing resources to enhance educational standards and practices. Applicants can find tools and guidance on the NYSED Charter School Office website. The grant encourages transparency and innovation in education. Application details and deadlines are provided in the funding opportunities section.
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to charter schools and organizations involved in public education improvement.
key_requirements: Must be a charter school or educational organization
Focus on improving public education
Adherence to NYSED standards and guidelines

grants.title: Extended School Day/School Violence Prevention (ESD/SVP) Program
grants.summary: Funds programs that provide extended school day activities and initiatives to prevent school violence.
grants.description: The Extended School Day/School Violence Prevention (ESD/SVP) Program supports schools and organizations in implementing programs that extend learning opportunities beyond the regular school day while addressing school violence prevention. Eligible applicants include schools and community-based organizations that aim to improve school climate and safety. Applications must demonstrate how the program will benefit students and align with state education priorities. Interested parties can apply through the New York State Education Department's official channels.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include schools and community-based organizations focused on extended learning and violence prevention.
sdg_alignment: SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
key_requirements: Programs must extend learning opportunities beyond the regular school day.
Initiatives must address school violence prevention.
Applicants must align programs with state education priorities.

grants.title: IDEA Grant Information
grants.summary: Funds programs and services for students with disabilities under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA).
grants.description: The IDEA Grant supports initiatives aimed at improving educational outcomes for students with disabilities across New York State. It provides funding for technical assistance, professional development, and monitoring to ensure equal opportunities and positive results for these students. Eligible applicants include schools and organizations that serve students with disabilities. Applications can be submitted through the New York State Education Department's website.
grants.eligibility_notes: Schools and organizations serving students with disabilities are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Applicants must serve students with disabilities.
Programs must align with IDEA objectives.
Applications must be submitted through the NYSED website.

grants.title: Perkins Federal Funds
grants.summary: Funds to improve career and technical education programs for high skill, high wage, high demand careers.
grants.description: Perkins Federal Funds are allocated to enhance career and technical education programs that prepare students for high skill, high wage, and high demand careers. Eligible agencies must meet federal requirements for program size, scope, and quality. These funds aim to support educational institutions in providing better resources and training for students entering the workforce. Applications can be submitted through the appropriate channels as outlined by the New York State Education Department.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible agencies must meet federal requirements for program size, scope, and quality.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Meet federal requirements for program size, scope, and quality
Focus on career and technical education
Prepare students for high skill, high wage, high demand careers

grants.title: Regents Charters and Regents Certificates of Incorporation for Education Corporations
grants.summary: This grant supports the incorporation of educational institutions through Regents charters or certificates of incorporation.
grants.description: The New York State Education Department provides authority to incorporate educational institutions as education corporations through Regents charters or certificates of incorporation. Eligible institutions include colleges, universities, schools, libraries, museums, historical societies, and cultural agencies. Applications must follow guidelines provided by the Board of Regents under Education Law §216. Institutions are subject to the Not-for-Profit Corporation Law as outlined in Education Law §216-a.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible organizations include educational institutions such as colleges, universities, schools, libraries, museums, historical societies, and cultural agencies.
key_requirements: Must be an educational institution
Subject to Education Law §216 and §216-a
Follow guidelines provided by the Board of Regents
